pendingTriage.author: plasticator2
pendingTriage.contentPreview: sti Thanks for the prompt reply! The story so far. Main listening room is the lounge, Pi 4B player/server, output through Hifiberry Digi2 pro SPDIF to external DAC. Squeezelite ALSA device name is hw:CARD=sndrpihifiberry,DEV=0. No replay gain. I already tried all permutations, but repeated today wit
pendingTriage.draft:
plasticator2 That's a really interesting finding — thank you for the systematic testing! The fact that syncing the Pi to the Windows/Squeezelite-X player produces perfect audio is a valuable clue.

This strongly suggests the issue is specific to the [B]HifiBerry Digi2 Pro / SPDIF output path[/B] on the Pi, rather than SpotOn's audio pipeline itself. A few thoughts:

[B]Most likely culprit: Sample rate or bit depth mismatch[/B]
SPDIF is strict about clock rates. If SpotOn is sending a sample rate that the HifiBerry or your DAC isn't expecting (or can't negotiate cleanly), you'll get exactly the kind of loud/distorted output you're describing. This can happen even if the same setup works fine with local files.

Can you check:
[CODE]
cat /proc/asound/sndrpihifiberry/pcm0p/sub0/hw_params
[/CODE]
...while audio is actively playing via SpotOn? This will show the actual sample rate and format being sent to the device.

[B]Things worth trying:[/B]
[LIST]
[*]In SpotOn player settings, try forcing [B]PCM format at 44100Hz[/B] if there's a rate option — this gives the SPDIF chain least room to misbehave
[*]Check if your DAC has a sample rate indicator — does it show a sane value during playback?
[*]Try setting Squeezelite's [B]-r 44100[/B] flag to lock the output rate
[/LIST]

[B]The log file[/B] — could you paste the relevant section here or attach it? I want to look for any resampling or format negotiation errors around the point playback starts.

If this turns out to be a reproducible bug, please do open a report at [URL]https://github.com/stiefenm/spoton/issues[/URL] with the hw_params output and log — it'll help track it properly.

pendingTriage.author: Andy_NO
pendingTriage.contentPreview: Originally posted by CJS 					 						 						 							 							 							 							 								 								 								 								 								 									 										 									 								 							 						 					 				 				I don't know what has gone wrong with your LMS, but I suggest that you manually uninstall the SpotOn plugin
pendingTriage.draft:
Hi Andy_NO,

Thanks for the extra details — piCorePlayer is the key piece of information here!

piCorePlayer works differently from a standard Linux install. The filesystem is [B]read-only by default[/B], and changes only persist if they are saved to the SD card before a reboot. This is almost certainly why you're stuck in a reboot loop — LMS is writing the plugin update flag, but when it restarts, the change hasn't been saved and it sees the old state again.

Here's what to try:

[B]1. After reinstalling/updating SpotOn, before rebooting:[/B]
Go to the piCorePlayer web interface → [B]Main Page → Backup[/B] (or "Save" depending on your version) and make sure you save the current state to SD card first.

[B]2. The reboot loop / checkbox unchecking[/B] is a classic piCorePlayer symptom when LMS changes aren't being persisted. The plugin appears installed (and even has its own menu) because it was loaded into memory, but the plugin list metadata isn't saved.

[B]3. Manual cleanup on piCorePlayer[/B] is a bit different — the Cache path may be under [CODE]/home/tc/.local/share/LMS/Cache[/CODE] or similar. Check your piCorePlayer LMS Information page for the exact path.

It may also be worth checking the [URL=https://forums.slimdevices.com]piCorePlayer forum threads[/URL] for LMS plugin persistence tips specific to your version (v11.1).

If after saving state properly the issue continues, please grab a [B]Diagnostics bundle[/B] from SpotOn Settings → Diagnostics and open a bug report at:
[URL]https://github.com/stiefenm/spoton/issues[/URL]

Good luck — this sounds more like a piCorePlayer persistence issue than a SpotOn bug specifically, but happy to help debug further!

pendingTriage.author: CJS
pendingTriage.contentPreview: Originally posted by Andy_NO 					 						 						 							 							 							 							 								 								 								 								 								 									 										 									 								 							 						 					 				 				EDIT: Spoton is version 2.3.7 and constantly asks for updating. Screenshot is in German but I think you
pendingTriage.draft:
[QUOTE=CJS]I get the point, but have no idea why on your LMS updating to the latest SpotOn plugin version apparently does not work.[/QUOTE]

Good troubleshooting steps, CJS. A few additional thoughts for Andy_NO:

The behaviour you're describing — checkbox unchecking itself after restart — is sometimes caused by a permissions issue on the plugin cache folders, or by LMS failing silently during the download/install step. The update process needs to be able to write to both [B]Cache/DownloadedPlugins[/B] and [B]Cache/InstalledPlugins[/B].

Andy_NO, a few things worth checking:

[B]1. Verify the currently installed version[/B]
As CJS suggests, check [CODE]install.xml[/CODE] directly. If it still reads 2.3.7 after repeated update attempts, the download itself may be failing before it can overwrite anything.

[B]2. Check folder permissions[/B]
On pCP, run:
[CODE]ls -la /mnt/mmcblk0p2/tce/slimserver/Cache/InstalledPlugins/Plugins/SpotOn/[/CODE]
Make sure the LMS process user has write access.

[B]3. Check the LMS server log[/B]
Look for any errors around the time you attempt the update — there may be a download failure or checksum mismatch being logged silently. The log is usually at [B]Logs/server.log[/B] in your LMS data folder.

[B]4. Manual install as a fallback[/B]
If the auto-update continues to fail, you can download the latest release directly from the [URL=https://github.com/stiefenm/spoton/releases]SpotOn GitHub releases page[/URL], stop LMS, replace the contents of the SpotOn plugin folder manually, then restart.

If none of that resolves it, please open a ticket at [URL=https://github.com/stiefenm/spoton/issues]https://github.com/stiefenm/spoton/issues[/URL] with your LMS version, pCP version, and a copy of the relevant server log entries — that will make it much easier to track down.
